paper_title: Paternity Analysis of the Olive Variety “Istrska Belica” and Identification of Pollen Donors by Microsatellite Markers

methods.method_name: Genetic Variability and Information Content Calculation
methods.description: This method calculates various parameters of genetic variability and information content for both offspring and parental populations using genotyping data with microsatellites.

steps.description: Observed heterozygosity (Ho) was obtained as the ratio among heterozygous individuals and the total number of genotypes for each locus.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate observed heterozygosity for each locus

steps.description: Expected heterozygosity (He) was estimated according to the formula He = 1−∑ p_i^2, where p_i is the frequency of the ith allele at the studied locus.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate expected heterozygosity using allele frequencies

steps.description: The effective number of alleles (Ne) was obtained according to the formula Ne = (∑ p_i^2)^−1.
Probability of identity (PI) was calculated using the formula to show the probability of wrongly assigning a genotype as the pollen donor.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate probability of identity for genotypes

steps.description: Exclusion probability (EP) was calculated according to published formula, which measures the ability of the marker to exclude a given relationship.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate exclusion probability for genetic markers

steps.description: Polymorphic information content (PIC) was calculated as 1 − ∑ p_i^2 − ∑ ∑ 2p_i^2p_j^2, where p_i equals the frequency of the ith allele and p_j the frequency of the (i + 1)th allele.
Ho, He, PIC, and PI were calculated using the IDENTITY 1.0 program for the analysis of microsatellite data and EP values were computed using FaMoz software.
steps.searchable_summary: use IDENTITY 1.0 and FaMoz software for calculations

methods.description: This method performs paternity analysis using the FaMoz software to identify the most likely pollen donor for the olive variety 'Istrska Belica' based on microsatellite markers.

steps.description: Paternity analysis was performed using the software program FaMoz. Genotypes of embryos and available genotypes for the 26 reference olive varieties were used to calculate LOD scores.
steps.searchable_summary: perform paternity analysis using FaMoz to calculate LOD scores

steps.description: FaMoz uses the genotypes of offspring, mother, and potential fathers to calculate the log of the odds ratio scores for any potential parentage relationship.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate log of odds ratio scores for parentage relationships

steps.description: The genotype with the highest LOD score above the threshold value for a given parent/offspring pair is considered the most likely pollen donor.
steps.searchable_summary: identify most likely pollen donor based on LOD scores

steps.description: In order to obtain the threshold value of the LOD score to identify true father, a simulation was done using 1000 generated offspring from the genotyped parents.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate offspring to determine LOD score threshold

steps.description: Since mistyping is very likely to occur when scoring microsatellite alleles, the error rate was set to 0.01-both in the simulation and in the assignment of the most likely father.
steps.searchable_summary: set error rate for microsatellite allele scoring
